headline: Case Studies
description: A curated selection of real QA engineering engagements. Each project demonstrates a specific discipline — automation, manual testing, API validation, accessibility, or AI system QA.

projects.title: SauceDemo E2E Selenium Automation Suite
projects.summary: Designed and implemented a production-ready Selenium + Pytest automation framework for SauceDemo using Page Object Model architecture, explicit waits, Allure reporting, and GitHub Actions CI/CD.
whatCovered: Built Selenium + Pytest framework from scratch using Page Object Model
Created a BasePage class to centralize explicit waits and eliminate flakiness
Structured six dedicated page objects for clean test-layer separation
Integrated Allure reporting and GitHub Actions for automated CI execution
impact: Eliminated 100% of implicit wait usage — zero flaky tests in CI pipeline
Structured coverage across login, cart, checkout, and product sorting flows
Regression suite runs end-to-end automatically on every push via GitHub Actions
POM architecture makes adding new tests fast with minimal boilerplate

detailContent.overview: SauceDemo is a publicly available e-commerce demo app. I used it as a real automation target to build a production-grade framework from scratch — the same way you would inside a QA team. The goal was to demonstrate POM architecture, explicit wait strategy, CI integration, and Allure reporting as a unified, working system.
stepsFollowed: Analyzed SauceDemo's page structure and identified six core page objects.
Built a BasePage class to centralize WebDriverWait logic and reusable element interactions.
Wrote modular Pytest test cases for login, product sorting, cart, and checkout flows.
Configured a GitHub Actions workflow to trigger the full suite on every commit push.

challengesFaced.challenge: Timing-related flakiness on dynamic UI elements during test runs.
challengesFaced.solution: Centralized all waits inside BasePage using explicit WebDriverWait with expected conditions — removed all implicit waits from the entire codebase.

challengesFaced.challenge: Allure reports not rendering correctly in the GitHub Actions CI environment.
challengesFaced.solution: Configured the Allure CLI path explicitly in the workflow YAML and added artifact upload steps so reports are accessible after each run.

resultsAndMetrics: Full suite runs in under 3 minutes covering all core e-commerce user flows.
Allure reports generate with step-level traceability after every CI execution.
Zero test failures due to timing issues after the BasePage explicit wait refactor.

projects.title: MERN Chat Application — Full Manual QA Engagement
projects.summary: End-to-end manual QA engagement on a MERN stack chat application — designing 29 test cases, executing them systematically, and reporting 4 real defects including a JWT session persistence vulnerability and a WebSocket zombie connection bug.
whatCovered: Designed 29 structured test cases covering auth, messaging, and real-time chat behavior
Built a Requirements Traceability Matrix (RTM) linking every test case to a requirement
Executed exploratory testing to uncover edge cases beyond written test cases
Reported 4 critical and high-severity defects with full reproduction steps
impact: Discovered a JWT session persistence vulnerability — session remained active post-logout
Identified a WebSocket zombie connection causing silent message delivery failures
Achieved 100% requirement coverage across all tested modules via RTM
Delivered a complete artifact suite: test plan, test cases, RTM, bug reports, and summary

detailContent.overview: A MERN stack real-time chat application used as a QA practice target. I conducted a full manual QA engagement — from test planning to defect reporting — treating it exactly as a real-world assignment with professional-grade deliverables a hiring team could evaluate directly.
stepsFollowed: Reviewed the application's feature set and created a structured test plan.
Designed 29 test cases with preconditions, test data, execution steps, and expected results.
Built a Requirements Traceability Matrix to confirm full coverage before execution.
Executed all test cases and ran exploratory sessions to probe edge-case behavior.

challengesFaced.challenge: WebSocket behavior is invisible through the UI alone — hard to observe or assert.
challengesFaced.solution: Used browser DevTools Network tab to monitor WebSocket frames directly, which revealed a zombie connection persisting after the chat session ended.

challengesFaced.challenge: The JWT session bug required precise logout timing to reproduce consistently.
challengesFaced.solution: Documented exact reproduction steps with action sequence and timing so developers could reproduce it reliably without back-and-forth.

resultsAndMetrics: 4 defects reported — 2 critical severity (security), 1 high, 1 medium — all fully reproducible.
JWT session vulnerability and WebSocket zombie connection both confirmed by developer review.
Complete professional artifact suite that mirrors real QA team output delivered.

projects.title: Restful-Booker REST API Test Suite
projects.summary: Designed and executed a structured REST API testing suite for the Restful-Booker public API using Python Requests and Pytest — covering full CRUD operations, authentication flows, and schema validation.
whatCovered: Scaffolded a clean project structure with base URL config and reusable request helpers
Wrote CRUD test cases covering Create, Read, Update, and Delete booking endpoints
Validated response status codes, JSON schema structure, and field-level data integrity
Tested token-based authentication and authorization behavior on protected endpoints
impact: Full CRUD coverage across all primary Restful-Booker API endpoints
Reusable conftest.py fixture handles auth token generation automatically across tests
Schema assertions catch silent API contract breaks without manual inspection
Modular test structure allows new endpoint tests to be added with minimal setup

detailContent.overview: Restful-Booker is a publicly available REST API built for QA practice. I used it to build a structured Python-based API test suite from scratch — simulating the kind of setup a QA engineer would build for a real backend service, with proper project scaffolding, reusable helpers, and schema-level assertions.
stepsFollowed: Explored all endpoints using Postman to understand schemas, request formats, and auth flows.
Scaffolded the project with a base config module for host URL and auth token management.
Wrote Pytest test cases for each CRUD operation with explicit status code and schema assertions.
Created a conftest.py fixture for token generation to eliminate repeated auth setup per test.

challengesFaced.challenge: Managing authentication tokens across multiple tests without duplicating setup code.
challengesFaced.solution: Created a session-scoped Pytest fixture in conftest.py that generates a fresh auth token once and injects it into any test that requires authorization.

resultsAndMetrics: Full CRUD coverage for the booking resource with status code and schema validation per endpoint.
Auth fixture eliminates manual token setup — any new test requiring auth needs zero extra code.
Project structure mirrors a real-world API test framework, reviewable end-to-end on GitHub.

projects.title: Wikipedia WCAG 2.1 Accessibility Audit
projects.summary: Conducted a structured manual accessibility audit of Wikipedia's core pages against WCAG 2.1 Level A and AA success criteria — evaluating keyboard navigation, color contrast, ARIA usage, and focus management.
whatCovered: Audited against WCAG 2.1 Level A and AA success criteria across core page types
Tested keyboard-only navigation and focus order across search, article, and edit flows
Evaluated color contrast ratios and heading hierarchy using accessibility tools
Reviewed ARIA landmark usage, alt text completeness, and screen reader compatibility
impact: Documented accessibility gaps across search, article, and edit page user flows
Identified focus management issues directly affecting keyboard-only users
Produced a structured audit report with WCAG criterion reference and severity per finding
Directly applicable experience for QA roles in healthcare, government, and public-sector projects

detailContent.overview: Wikipedia is one of the most visited websites in the world, making accessibility critical for millions of users — including those relying on screen readers, keyboard navigation, or assistive technology. I conducted a structured WCAG 2.1 audit across key page types, treating it as a real-world accessibility QA engagement with a formal deliverable.
stepsFollowed: Identified key page types for audit scope: homepage, search results, article view, and edit page.
Mapped relevant WCAG 2.1 Level A and AA success criteria to each page type.
Tested using keyboard-only navigation, evaluating tab order and focus indicator visibility.
Used Lighthouse and browser DevTools to verify contrast ratios and ARIA landmark structure.

challengesFaced.challenge: WCAG criteria can be ambiguous — pass/fail is not always immediately clear.
challengesFaced.solution: Cross-referenced the official WCAG Understanding documents and Deque's accessibility guidelines for each finding to ensure each verdict was objectively defensible.

resultsAndMetrics: Structured audit report produced with WCAG criterion ID, severity, and affected element per finding.
Hands-on experience applying WCAG 2.1 evaluation methodology to a real production website.
Directly applicable to QA roles in healthcare, government, and compliance-sensitive projects.

projects.title: Call RAG System — AI Script Generation (Doveriye Internship)
projects.summary: Sole technical contributor at Doveriye (Aug–Nov 2025) — built a Python-based AI Call RAG System using vector databases and a local LLM for automated script generation, while simultaneously executing manual test cases and documenting real bugs found in the system.
whatCovered: Built an AI-powered call script generation app using Python, vector DB, and a local LLM
Designed and executed manual test cases covering the full RAG pipeline end-to-end
Documented real bugs found during internal QA with reproduction steps and severity
Managed full development and QA responsibilities as the sole technical contributor
impact: Delivered a functional RAG system from zero to working product within 4 months
Real production bugs identified and documented before system reached end users
Built practical Python proficiency from scratch through a live production project
Bridged developer and QA roles within a single real-world product engagement

detailContent.overview: At Doveriye, I was the sole technical contributor responsible for building a Call RAG System — an AI application that generates call scripts by retrieving relevant context from a vector database and feeding it to a local LLM. Alongside development, I also wore the QA hat: designing manual test cases for the pipeline and documenting real bugs found during internal testing.
stepsFollowed: Designed the RAG pipeline architecture — vector document ingestion, retrieval query logic, and LLM prompt flow.
Implemented the system end-to-end using Python with a vector database for context storage and retrieval.
Wrote manual test cases to validate retrieval accuracy, LLM output structure, and edge-case inputs.
Documented each bug found during self-testing with clear reproduction steps and expected vs actual behavior.

challengesFaced.challenge: Building an end-to-end Python system with no prior Python experience.
challengesFaced.solution: Learned by building — started with the smallest working version of each component, then iterated. Used the project itself as the curriculum rather than studying in isolation.

challengesFaced.challenge: Testing an AI system where generated output quality is inherently subjective.
challengesFaced.solution: Defined objective, verifiable test criteria around retrieval accuracy, response latency, and output format structure — rather than evaluating open-ended generated text subjectively.

resultsAndMetrics: Delivered a fully functional Call RAG System within the 4-month internship window.
Real bugs identified, documented, and resolved before the system reached end users.
Internship provided foundational Python + AI system experience that directly underpins the automation portfolio.
